title: The Shepherd Who Carries Me Home
subtitle: Christ goes out for the one lost sheep and bears it back on the wood of the Cross

stillness: Be still, wretched sinner, and draw near.
for Christ is all things to us.
Come, and bring Him your wound.
gospel: The publicans and sinners were drawing near to Him,
drawing near to Jesus the Saviour, to hear Him.
And the Pharisees and Scribes murmured,
saying: This man receives sinners, and eats with them.
And He spoke to them this parable:
What man among you who has a hundred sheep,
if he lose one of them,
does he not leave the ninety-nine in the desert,
and go after that which was lost, until he find it?
And when he has found it,
he lays it upon his shoulders, rejoicing,
and coming home he calls together his friends and neighbours,
saying to them: Rejoice with me, for I have found my sheep which was lost.
I say to you, there shall be joy in heaven
more than over ninety-nine just who need no penance.
behold: Attend now to each thing as if you were present.
Behold the good Shepherd, who finds the lost sheep,
and because for weakness it could not walk,
He did not strike it, did not beat it,
did not drive it harshly back to the flock;
but humbly laying it upon His shoulders,
and bearing it gently, He carried it home.
Behold: the shoulders of Christ are the arms of the Cross.
There I laid down my sins;
upon that noble gibbet, that Cross, I found rest.
And mark well that the Shepherd laid the sheep upon His shoulders rejoicing:
for though the pain of His Passion and death was great,
He bore that labour and that sorrow gladly,
to win back the sheep that was lost.
consider: Consider: you are the one sheep that strayed and perished,
when, by sinning, man forsook the pastures of life.
And in one man's straying, the whole race of men went astray.
You are simple before so cunning an enemy, the devil;
I have gone astray like a sheep that was lost; seek your servant.
When the Shepherd took the sheep upon His shoulders,
He took up our human nature, and Himself bore our sins;
in His arms stretched wide upon the Cross
He bore the punishment due to our sins.
And finding the sheep, He came home;
for, man being repaired, He returned to the heavenly kingdom,
and with the sheep He ascended, leading captivity captive.
He did not punish, did not beat, did not drive you;
He bore you. This is how God receives the sinner who repents.
prayer: Come, Lord Jesus, seek your servant;
come, good Shepherd, seek your straying and weary sheep;
come, Spouse of Mother Church, seek the lost coin;
come, Father of mercy, receive the prodigal son returning to you.
Come, not with the rod, but with love, and in the spirit of gentleness;
come then, Lord, for you alone can call back the one who strays,
find the one who is lost, reconcile the one who has fled;
come, that you may work salvation on earth and joy in heaven;
and grant me to do true and perfect penance,
that I may be to the Angels an occasion of gladness,
O Lord God of my salvation. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Et, secundum Ambrosium, humeri Christi crucis brachia sunt: illic mea peccata deposui, in illa patibuli nobilis cruce quievi.
latinJewel.english: And, according to Ambrose, the shoulders of Christ are the arms of the Cross: there I laid down my sins; upon that noble gibbet, that Cross, I found rest.
